SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.query

Class InsertQuery

java.lang.Object
org.javalabs.jpa.query.DatabaseQuery
org.javalabs.jpa.query.WriteQuery
org.javalabs.jpa.query.InsertQuery
All Implemented Interfaces:
Query
public class InsertQuery extends WriteQuery
Insert query that will handle all insert operation.

A call to EntityManager.persist(java.lang.Object) will call this query object to persist the record. InsertQuery class internally maintains a cache to hold the in-transit entities, i.e., the one to be persisted. Once the cache is full, or user has explicitly issued a EntityManager.flush(), the in-memory record(s) will be persisted in underlying database. The cache is introduced in order to reduce the number of DB calls, thus improving the overall performance of the system.

Instance of this class is not threadsafe. The standard practice is, one EntityManager will always have a single instance of InsertQuery.

Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

    • prepare

      protected List<List<Object>> prepare(PreparedStatement pStmt) throws SQLException
      Description copied from class: WriteQuery
      Prepare the statement. Preparing the statement essentially means, replacing the question mark/ place holders with the bind variables. Different type of queries have their own strategy of replacing the bind variables. This API will iterate through the the entities added so far to extract the column values. After successful replace, this API will return the list containing the bind variables.
      Specified by:
      prepare in class WriteQuery
      Parameters:
      pStmt - Current prepared statement being executed.
      Returns:
      List
      Throws:
      SQLException
